method: Index-strategy verification via faithful mirror tables (real index DDL) seeded at production-scale volume, then EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) on hot-path query shapes. Bench harness compiled. Live HTTP latency NOT captured (see caveats).
caveats: Query timings below are DB-layer planner+execution times on a single warm connection, NOT end-to-end HTTP P50/P95/P99.

Live latency/QPS/memory require a built+running homeserver on :8008 with BENCH_ADMIN_TOKEN and seeded scenario data; none available this run (server unreachable, live DB near-empty: 8 memberships / 0 device_keys).

The two cargo bench targets are thin HTTP wrappers that probe /_matrix/client/versions and SKIP when no server is up; they do not self-seed the 8 requested scenarios and hit hardcoded rooms (!test:localhost).
Mirror tables carry the exact production index definitions but no FK/trigger overhead, so INSERT/write-amplification costs are not represented.

slo_targets_from_harness.note: Benches emit per-iteration timings and do NOT hard-fail on SLO breach (decoupled from target cluster).

index_existence.verdict: ALL rule-十二 hot-path indexes physically present
index_existence.source: pg_indexes on live docker synapse DB (251 tables, full v10 schema)
index_existence.note: presence_subscriptions has only its PK (subscriber_id, target_id) serving as index — matches audit-03 §3 column-drift note (rule text says user_id/observed_user_id).

explain_verification.note: Mirror tables at volume, ANALYZE'd. exec_ms = EXPLAIN ANALYZE Execution Time (DB layer). All shapes use the intended index; ZERO seq scans.

queries.sql_shape: SELECT user_id,membership FROM room_memberships WHERE room_id=$1 AND membership='join'
SELECT event_id,sender,event_type,content,origin_server_ts FROM events WHERE room_id=$1 AND stream_ordering>$2 ORDER BY stream_ordering DESC LIMIT 100
queries.observation: planner chose narrow idx_events_room_stream_ordering over idx_events_sync_covering; covering index not winning at LIMIT 100 (heap fetch only 3 buffers). Re-evaluate covering index value under larger LIMIT / high concurrency.

queries.sql_shape: SELECT ... FROM events WHERE room_id=$1 ORDER BY origin_server_ts DESC LIMIT 50
SELECT max(stream_ordering) FROM events WHERE room_id=$1
SELECT user_id,device_id,key_json FROM device_keys WHERE user_id=ANY($1)
SELECT ... FROM one_time_keys WHERE user_id=$1 AND device_id=$2 AND algorithm=$3 AND used=false LIMIT 1
queries.plan: Index Scan idx_one_time_keys_user_device (Filter: NOT used AND algorithm)

queries.sql_shape: SELECT data_type,content FROM account_data WHERE user_id=$1

scenarios_not_db_measurable_this_run.7_media_10mb_upload_download: I/O + storage path, not an index shape; needs running server
scenarios_not_db_measurable_this_run.8_federation_server_key_query: server_keys table lookup, small; needs running server (or seed server_keys)

cached: sliding sync window snapshot + E2EE stream pos (filters.rs:89, extensions.rs:211 via cache.set_raw/get_raw)
NOT_cached_hot_reads_P1: sliding_sync_service/state.rs:18 get_state_events(room) (full room state per request)
hot_path_cache_inventory.note: SyncService has NO shared SharedInfra.cache field (sync_service/mod.rs:45 only a local lazy_loaded_members_cache HashMap). Index hits are fast, but /sync issues these uncached reads every request — caching filter/account_data/max_stream_id/room_state is the highest-leverage optimization (audit-04 P1).

next_steps_for_full_latency_baseline: Build+run release server against docker DB (DB_PASSWORD=synapse, port 15432->5432).
Seed scenario data (1000-member room, 100 users w/ device keys, 10MB media) — no seed script exists; write one.
Register admin, export BENCH_ADMIN_TOKEN + BENCH_BASE_URL, run cargo bench for real P50/P95/P99 + QPS.
Add pg_stat_statements + per-endpoint query-count assertions to catch N+1 regressions (audit-03 flagged device delete_devices_batch N+1).
